title: Oncostema elongata

The classification of Oncostema elongata is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, the clade Eudicots, the order Malpighiales, the family Phyllanthaceae, the subfamily Phyllanthoideae, and the genus Oncostema. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships among different species of plants. intro: Oncostema elongata, also known as the "False Asafoetida" or "False Hing", is a plant species in the family Loranthaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate respiratory issues and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Oncostema elongata include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
